AFPoignard_PGDL3.overview: The Poignard is a light aerospace fighter produced by Kallon Industries, a company better known for its DropShip manufacturing operations in the Loyalty system. Designed in the years following the Jihad as an interceptor capable of engaging modern threat fighters, the Poignard represents Kallon's first foray into aerospace fighter production, leveraging fabrication expertise and supply chains developed through decades of spacecraft construction. Entering service in 3083 with the Marik-Stewart Commonwealth, the design proved capable enough to earn sales to the Republic of the Sphere within two years, establishing Kallon as a credible player in the light aerospace fighter market. The Poignard is a fast, aggressively armed platform for its weight class, designed to close quickly and deliver concentrated medium-range firepower against both air and ground targets.
AFPoignard_PGDL3.capabilities:
The Poignard relies on a Magna 245 XL engine to achieve exceptional acceleration for a light fighter, reaching a safe sustained thrust of 4.5 Gs with a maximum burst of 7 Gs. Kallon's engineers caution against sustained full-thrust operation: exceeding sixty-five percent throttle risks damaging the Aeroframe XSR structure, and a complete 7-G burn can damage the craft outright and injure the pilot. This ceiling places practical limits on combat maneuvering, requiring disciplined throttle management even in close engagements.

The fighter's primary offensive punch comes from four Magna Mk VI extended-range medium lasers mounted in underwing pods, split evenly across both wings. These weapons, which can be mistaken visually for external ordnance stores, give the Poignard an approximately 200-kilometer engagement range in space operations. Supporting them is a nose-mounted Imperator Napoleon Light AC/5 carrying twenty bursts of ammunition, providing the sole ballistic option and an additional damage tier at shorter ranges. A single Martell Small Pulse Laser occupies the nose alongside the autocannon, serving a point-defense function, though its forward placement limits effectiveness in direct head-to-head engagements.

Coordinating the entire weapons suite is the Kallon Victory Tracker Delta fire control system, which manages all five weapons simultaneously while operating the Martell independently in point-defense mode. Five programmable firing modes allow pilots to tailor engagement profiles to mission requirements. Approach vector, armor status, and velocity data are surfaced through the throttle interface, keeping the control stick uncluttered during high-G maneuvering. Ten tons of Kallon AeroWeave II ferro-aluminum armor provide solid protection for a 35-ton design, and eleven double heat sinks keep the energy loadout comfortably managed under sustained fire.
AFPoignard_PGDL3.history:
Kallon Industries' production history on Loyalty extends back to DropShip fabrication, with its orbital yards responsible for the Kuan-Ti and Vengeance-class vessels before the Jihad. When the Word of Blake's campaign reached the Marik-Stewart region, the neighboring SelaSys WarShip facility absorbed the majority of hostile attention, and Kallon's installations survived the war largely intact. This relatively favorable outcome left the company with an undamaged industrial base and a clear path to post-war expansion.

In the mid-3070s, Kallon extended its existing DropShip production infrastructure to incorporate a dedicated aerospace fighter manufacturing line. Intelligence gathered before the dissolution of the Free Worlds League had suggested Kallon was weighing a second aerospace line or a conventional fighter facility as additional capacity options. By 3083, the first Poignard fighters were rolling off the Loyalty production line, sold exclusively to the newly formed Marik-Stewart Commonwealth as that state rebuilt its military aviation assets. Sales volume over the following two years confirmed the design's viability in the emerging post-Jihad market.

In 3085, Kallon expanded its Asuncion facility with a second Poignard production line, dedicated to meeting Republic Armed Forces procurement requirements. The Republic had approached Kallon through the Marik-Stewart Commonwealth following the Loyalty line's commercial success, and negotiations were described as amicable by both parties. The L3 and R3 variants entered production alongside the Asuncion expansion, broadening the design's mission profile and providing the RAF and Commonwealth with more specialized options in addition to the general-purpose primary model.
AFPoignard_PGDL3.deployment: The L3 trades the Y3's mixed armament entirely for a pair of extended-range large lasers supplemented by additional heat sinking, optimized for engagements at distances where the primary model's medium-range weapons are ineffective. The L3 is best suited to pilots who prefer to engage at extended range, where it can threaten heavier fighters and suppress DropShip point defense before a main engagement. It performs poorly in close-range maneuvering fights where the Y3 is more capable.

AFStuka_STUK5.overview: The Stuka entered service with the Terran Hegemony in 2530, developed by Lycomb IntraTechnologies as a heavy aerospace fighter purpose-built for sustained combat operations. Where lighter designs trade armor for speed, the Stuka was engineered to endure, a ground attack platform capable of absorbing punishment as readily as it delivers it. When the newly formed Star League Defense Force demanded expanded production to support the Reunification War, the type proved its worth quickly, and the SLDF's investment in the design established a production legacy that would outlast the Star League itself. Federated Suns forces have long maintained a monopoly on its manufacture, a strategic advantage that shaped aerospace doctrine across the Inner Sphere for centuries. The fighter's silhouette became synonymous with Davion air supremacy, and its reputation for durability secured its place as one of the Inner Sphere's most recognizable and enduring heavy fighters.
AFStuka_STUK5.capabilities:
The Stuka is built around resilience and sustained firepower. At one hundred tons, it sits at the upper limit of heavy aerospace fighter classification, its Lycomb 11 frame designed to carry that mass in structural load capacity. A GM 300ATF power plant drives the craft to respectable speeds for its weight class, while the Monitor 200 guidance system (integrated with the O/P 9000AT communications suite and O/P 2500TGFD targeting system) provides atmospheric maneuverability that belies the fighter's bulk.

The STU-K5's weapons loadout spans multiple ranges. The nose mounts a Simpson LRM-20 rack and Holly SRM-4 with a Martell medium laser, while each wing carries two Exostar large lasers providing the primary striking power at range. Two rear-facing Martell medium lasers deter pursuit. Fifteen tons of AeroScale Plus armor give the Stuka the durability to weather engagements that would destroy lighter designs, and thirty single heat sinks manage the wing laser heat load with reserve capacity for extended operations.

The trade-off is offensive punch relative to weight. A well-armed medium fighter can often outgun the Stuka at comparable ranges, and pilots are expected to compensate through superior durability and endurance rather than raw firepower. The design's strengths lie in its ability to absorb damage, remain on station, and sustain weapons output long after lighter opponents are forced to disengage.
AFStuka_STUK5.notes: This variant is unique to the MUL as it has the ER PPCs. But I've adjusted the dates to make things work.
AFStuka_STUK5.history:
Lycomb IntraTechnologies developed the Stuka in 2530 under contract to the Terran Hegemony, producing the initial run from facilities on Terra. The design proved capable enough that when the newly formed Star League Defense Force sought a heavy ground attack platform for the Reunification War, the Stuka was the natural choice. To meet SLDF demand, Lycomb opened a second production line on Demeter; a world that would prove central to the fighter's survival across the centuries that followed.

As the Star League fractured and the Amaris Civil War consumed the Inner Sphere, the Terra facility was destroyed during the Coup. The Federated Suns had quietly acquired control of the company's assets during the First Succession War and, acting on sound military foresight, had already begun duplicating the Demeter production infrastructure on New Avalon. When House Liao struck the Demeter facilities in 2795, hoping to deny the Armed Forces of the Federated Suns a critical supplier, the Capellan strike destroyed the factory but missed its strategic goal entirely. The New Avalon line was nearly complete. Within years, the renamed Lycomb-Davion IntroTech held the only active Stuka production in the Inner Sphere, a monopoly the Federated Suns would guard for generations.

The Stuka's combat record through the Succession Wars carved its reputation in stone. In 2899, a fighter wing of Stukas was at the center of the Fifteenth Sword of Light's combat drop over Quentin. The operation ended in disaster for the DCMS: the Stukas tore through the heavily laden Kurita DropShips as they struggled to maneuver in Quentin's upper atmosphere, and the Fifteenth was struck from the rolls. The battle carries an enduring historical mystery. The DCMS never publicly clarified which regiment the Fifteenth actually was, and the ISF apparently saw no reason to correct the record. Given that the DCMS had reduced its twelve Sword of Light regiments to five in 2740, the identity of the obliterated unit remains a deliberate gap in official history.

By 2930, Stukas were carrying out more conventional ground support operations. Avalon Hussars fighters employed them to bomb and strafe Liao defenders during the fighting on Lee, pressing Davion advances and contributing to the Federated Suns' capture of that world. The engagement became a textbook example of the Stuka's ground attack doctrine in practice.

In 2999, the type played a central role in a Davion intelligence operation. False information planted by House Davion agents lured a DCMS strike force into position at Kesai IV. When Kurita fighters returned from ground attack missions (low on fuel and ammunition) a waiting Stuka wing sprang the ambush and decimated the formation. Actions like this cemented the Stuka's reputation in Kurita military doctrine as a threat demanding dedicated countermeasures.

The Stuka's association with Wolf's Dragoons added an unusual chapter to its history. When the Draconis Combine attempted to forcibly absorb the mercenary command following the expiration of their contract, Stuka pilots loyal to the Dragoons flew escort for the evacuation of Dragoon dependents to the Federated Suns. One of the handful of non-Davion pilots operating the type flew an all-black Stuka that went on to destroy more Kurita fighters during the evacuation fighting than many Davion units had managed in prior engagements combined - a result the DCMS found particularly galling.

The Jihad brought significant damage to the New Avalon facilities. Lycomb-Davion IntroTech was operating at roughly one third of pre-war efficiency by 3079. Recovery came through the Castle Avalon reconstruction program, which directed substantial funding toward New Avalon's manufacturing base. With Johnston Industries struggling to restore full Dagger OmniFighter output, the AFFS prioritized the Stuka lines as a compensating measure. The later dissemination of the New Dallas Memory Core allowed the Royal STU-K5b (long lost with the Star League) to re-enter production, expanding the line's capabilities for the first time in generations.
AFStuka_STUK5.deployment: The STU-K5 serves as the backbone of Federated Suns aerospace operations throughout the Succession Wars and beyond. Deployed extensively by the Avalon Hussars, Crucis Lancers, and Draconis March Militia, it operates primarily in the ground attack and DropShip escort roles, leveraging its superior armor and range-flexible weapons to outlast opponents. A small number entered service with Wolf's Dragoons and other Federated Suns-aligned mercenary commands, where the type's reputation for durability made it a prized acquisition. House Davion's production monopoly ensured the K5 remained the dominant heavy fighter in their arsenal for over two centuries.

BASheduAssaultBattleArmor_SupportDermalS.overview: The Shedu is a battle armor design in the Word of Blake's Demon Series that is used exclusively by Manei Domini, one of the last of the Demons to be created by Precentor Vapula.
BASheduAssaultBattleArmor_SupportDermalS.capabilities: The major advantage of the quad chassis is its speed, and Vapula used that to give the Shedu an excellent maximum ground speed for an assault-weight suit. Unlike other quad designs like the Fenrir or Rottweiler however Vapula did not skimp on protection and gave it 600kg of advanced armor, enough to withstand a Gauss rifle slug. Thus compared to the Fenrir the Shedu has slightly less space and weight for weaponry, though since like all Demon suits it is designed to work in groups this is less of a problem. Although equipped with modular weapon mounts three primary configurations appeared with the introduction of the Shedu, followed by a couple more exotic variants. As one of the last Demon suits to enter service the Shedu was relatively rare, though more common than its companion the Nephilim.
BASheduAssaultBattleArmor_SupportDermalS.history: Named for the half-bull, half-human demons of myth, the Shedu is an assault-class, quadrupedal battle suit, a radical departure from the rest of the Demon line.
BASheduAssaultBattleArmor_SupportDermalS.deployment: One of the original variants of the Shedu the "Support" was designed with limited, short-range firepower in mind. This comes in the form of two One-Shot SRM-3s, which can make short work of light units or heavier units that have already been damaged. The staying power for this configuration comes from two Small Lasers. Combined the two weapons can take out squads of battle armor or endanger almost any light 'Mech.
BASheduAssaultBattleArmor_SupportDermalS.manufacturer: Gibson Federated BattleMechs (A Division of Free Worlds Defense Industries)

BASpectreStealthBattleArmor_Sqd1.overview: The Spectre Battle Armor was developed and fielded by Protectorate Arms Conglomerate.
BASpectreStealthBattleArmor_Sqd1.capabilities: The Spectre is pretty quick for a humanoid Battle Armor, but its Mechanical Jump Booster is not a true replacement for regular Jump Jets. Magnetic clamps adapted from the Fa Shih Battle Armor allow the Spectre to be carried into battle. While its armament gives it better combat ability than some early stealth suits, it still cannot stand up to larger battle armor like the Elemental or Kanazuchi.
BASpectreStealthBattleArmor_Sqd1.history: It has proliferated throughout the Periphery, reaching the Protectorate’s Filtvelt allies and the Raven Alliance in large numbers. Mercenaries have spread the Spectre throughout the rest of the Periphery and Inner Sphere. It has got its name from the Calderon Shield stealth armor that protects it. This armor, while good at hiding it from enemy sensors, fares very poorly when under fire.
BASpectreStealthBattleArmor_Sqd1.deployment: The Spectre Battle Armor sports a very limited armament of one "David" Light Gauss Rifle with 20 shots and a single Battle Vibro-Claw.
BASpectreStealthBattleArmor_Sqd1.manufacturer: Protectorate Arms Conglomerate (Formerly Protectorate Defense Conglomerate)

BMAlphaWolf_Aldris.overview: The Alpha Wolf, an agile Assault OmniMech engineered by Clan Wolf, marks the clan's third totemic 'Mech. Initially delayed due to a concentrated effort on rejuvenating the Wolf Touman, its production commenced at the dawn of 3150. Drawing inspiration and components from the Warwolf, Timber Wolf, and Wulfen, this unit epitomizes a paradigm shift in Clan military doctrine, notably with the integration of Stealth Armor, aiming for enhanced defensive capabilities amidst battlefield adversities.
BMAlphaWolf_Aldris.capabilities: Envisioned as an amalgam of the Warwolf and Timber Wolf within an assault 'Mech framework, the Alpha Wolf incorporates the stealth armor from the Wulfen. While the incorporation of stealth armor on an assault 'Mech initially garnered skepticism, its efficacy in scrambling enemy targeting systems, thereby prolonging the Alpha Wolf's survivability in direct confrontations, justified the design choice. This armor, rather than facilitating traditional stealth roles like scouting or infiltration, accentuates the Alpha Wolf's prowess in standup battles.
BMAlphaWolf_Aldris.history:
The journey of the Alpha Wolf began as a much-delayed venture, which could have seen the light of day much earlier if not for the intense focus on bolstering the Wolf Touman. The interim saw the production of the Stormwolf, with the Alpha Wolf only entering full production recently. However, the delay bore fruit as it allowed the Empire's scientists to react to rival advancements, like the Jade Phoenix, ensuring that when the Alpha Wolf was finally unveiled, it truly epitomized the pinnacle of Clan Wolf's engineering prowess.

The pre-production Alpha Wolf units were swiftly dispatched among front-line Clusters for field testing, showcasing a bid for potential independence from auxiliary services. The recent battle for Skye served as its trial by fire, where two new prototypes in the A configuration demonstrated exceptional combat efficacy. Their stellar performance against the Turkina Keshik and the Falcons validated the stealth armor's operational advantage, further manifested in subsequent encounters with a Jade Phoenix and a Shrike. The Alpha Wolf's deployments in the Republic's counterattack into Wolf Empire space and the confrontation on Gacrux further underscored its combat potential, marking it as a valuable asset in Clan Wolf's arsenal.
BMAlphaWolf_Aldris.deployment: Equipped with twin LRM-15 missile racks, the Alpha Wolf bombards adversaries as it narrows down the range, switching to its pair of class-5 rotary autocannons to shred through remaining armor. Although lacking in backup weaponry, the six tons of autocannon and four tons of missile ammunition ensure a fair endurance on the battlefield.

BMAlphaWolf_Luna.deployment: Tailored for sniping, it carries dual ER large lasers and two Extended LRM-10s to capitalize on the stealth armor's sensor-defeating properties. Three ER medium lasers provide backup, with six additional double heat sinks to manage the heat burden.

BMArcher_ARC6BC.overview: One of the most iconic BattleMechs is the Archer, with its low-slung torso, enormous fists, and missile-bay covers. The Archer was first manufactured in 2474 for heavy-hitting, long-range brawling, and fire-support.
BMArcher_ARC6BC.capabilities: Two enormous missile launchers with tons of ammo power the Archer. The missiles were formidable, but their short-range inaccuracy required four medium lasers, two rear-facing and one in each arm. Enlarged hand actuators allow the design to undertake severe physical attacks and transport captured supplies during raids. The cockpit of the Archer is situated beneath the center midsection, giving the pilot a unique battlefield view. The torso armor belt above the cockpit protects the gyro and engine from the superior armor. Many pilots initially entered battle with their missile bays locked to hide the 'Mech's real capabilities, leading some to believe it was a close-combat design. As the Archer became famous, this pretext became meaningless. Archer pilots rarely learn new skills, but this hasn't tarnished the 'Mech's reputation.
BMArcher_ARC6BC.history: The design was constructed at the request of Terran Hegemony's HAF for a new "heavy fire support BattleMech" and took nearly nine years to complete. This owed in part to the design team, which included former HAF MechWarriors, field technicians, and general officer advisors, and the company's engineer team, led by Supervising Engineer Linn Tommi, disagreeing over theoretical and practical concerns. The intricate engineering proposal presented to the HAF includes complicated, difficult-to-repair stabilizing technology and powerful, computer-slaved sensors designed to make the Archer the most accurate BattleMech in existence. However, the final prototype abandoned these criteria in favor of a sturdier, blockier, and more heavily armored frame that is still in use today. It was so effective that it remained the Hegemony standard for more than a decade and a half, until technological developments allowed Earthwerks to produce a more efficient variant. The Archer, which debuted in 2474, quickly became one of the most well-known and commonly deployed BattleMechs. The Archer was a contemporary of 'Mechs such as the Thunderbolt and the Banshee, and its primary job was fire support, though early in its combat career it was regarded an attack 'Mech capable of long-range brawling, and it was deployed in a variety of different duties. More than 100,000 Archers were built between its introduction and the outbreak of the First Succession War, with tens of thousands of variation types produced over the centuries. Though many were destroyed or deconstructed for spare components, they remained a common sight in the Great House troops, with six distinct manufacturers creating new Archers throughout the Succession Wars. The retrieval of the Helm Memory Core had a significant impact on the creation of the Archer. New variations were created that used rediscovered lostech, giving the venerable 'Mech a few new tricks to surprise its opponents. By the time of the Clan Invasion, most of the major manufacturers had switched over to building these new variants for the Free Worlds League, Federated Commonwealth, Free Rasalhague Republic, and Wolf's Dragoons; only Vandenberg Mechanized Industries continued to build original ARC-2R Archers for the Taurian Concordat. As technology advanced, newer, more advanced models of the Archer were produced.
BMArcher_ARC6BC.deployment: The Archer uses two Doombud LRM-20 missile launchers with four tons of ammo for two minutes of continuous fire. Side torsos shared ammo. Some Archer drivers closed their missile bay doors to confuse the adversary while the design was new, however this ploy no longer works. Archer carries four Diverse Optics Type 18 midrange lasers for close combat. Each arm has a laser, and the other two are positioned in a central turret above the cockpit, allowing them to attack targets behind the 'Mech. The Archer's large battlefists help it fight close-quarters and steal supplies during raids. The Archer cruised at 44.1 km/h and carried thirteen tons of armor. With only 10 heat sinks, an Archer firing both LRM launchers and lasers would quickly overheat. The Archer's popularity meant that most adversary pilots knew how to use it to their advantage. The Archer's cockpit, beneath the central torso, was its most distinctive feature. The Gyro and engine were protected by the torso armor belt, but this placement gave the MechWarrior a unique battlefield perspective.
BMArcher_ARC6BC.manufacturer: Earthwerks-FWL, Inc.,Bowie Industries,Diplass BattleMechs,Defiance Industries,LexaTech Industries,Vandenberg Mechanized Industries,Gorton, Kingsley, and Thorpe Enterprises,Earthwerks Incorporated

By the time of the Clan Invasion, most of the major manufacturers had switched over to building these new variants for the Free Worlds League, Federated Commonwealth, Free Rasalhague Republic, and Wolf's Dragoons; only Vandenberg Mechanized Industries continued to build original ARC-2R Archers for the Taurian Concordat. As technology advanced, newer, more advanced models of the Archer were produced.
BMArcher_ARC6MC.deployment: The Archer uses two Doombud LRM-20 missile launchers with four tons of ammo for two minutes of continuous fire. Side torsos shared ammo. Some Archer drivers closed their missile bay doors to confuse the adversary while the design was new, however this ploy no longer works. Archer carries four Diverse Optics Type 18 midrange lasers for close combat. Each arm has a laser, and the other two are positioned in a central turret above the cockpit, allowing them to attack targets behind the 'Mech. The Archer's large battlefists help it fight close-quarters and steal supplies during raids. The Archer cruised at 44.1 km/h and carried thirteen tons of armor. With only 10 heat sinks, an Archer firing both LRM launchers and lasers would quickly overheat. The Archer's popularity meant that most adversary pilots knew how to use it to their advantage. The Archer's cockpit, beneath the central torso, was its most distinctive feature. The Gyro and engine were protected by the torso armor belt, but this placement gave the MechWarrior a unique battlefield perspective.
BMArcher_ARC6MC.manufacturer: Earthwerks-FWL, Inc.,Bowie Industries,Diplass BattleMechs,Defiance Industries,LexaTech Industries,Vandenberg Mechanized Industries,Gorton, Kingsley, and Thorpe Enterprises,Earthwerks Incorporated

BMArcher_ARCAGC.overview: One of the most iconic BattleMechs is the Archer, with its low-slung torso, enormous fists, and missile-bay covers. The Archer was first manufactured in 2474 for heavy-hitting, long-range brawling, and fire-support.
BMArcher_ARCAGC.capabilities: Two enormous missile launchers with tons of ammo power the Archer. The missiles were formidable, but their short-range inaccuracy required four medium lasers, two rear-facing and one in each arm. Enlarged hand actuators allow the design to undertake severe physical attacks and transport captured supplies during raids. The cockpit of the Archer situated beneath the center midsection, giving the pilot a unique battlefield view. The torso armor belt above the cockpit protects the gyro and engine from the superior armor. Many pilots initially entered battle with their missile bays locked to hide the 'Mech's real capabilities, leading some to believe it was a close-combat design. As the Archer became famous, this pretext became meaningless. Archer pilots rarely learn new skills, but this hasn't tarnished the 'Mech's reputation.
BMArcher_ARCAGC.history: The design was constructed at the request of Terran Hegemony's HAF for a new "heavy fire support BattleMech" and took nearly nine years to complete. This was owing in part to the design team, which included former HAF MechWarriors, field technicians, and general officer advisors, and the company's engineer team, led by Supervising Engineer Linn Tommi, disputing over theoretical and practical concerns. The intricate engineering proposal presented to the HAF includes complicated, difficult-to-repair stabilizing technology and powerful, computer-slaved sensors designed to make the Archer the most accurate BattleMech in existence. However, the final prototype abandoned these criteria in favor of a sturdier, blockier, and more heavily armored frame that is still in use today. It was so effective that it remained the Hegemony standard for more than a decade and a half, until technological developments allowed Earthwerks to produce a more efficient variant. The Archer, which debuted in 2474, quickly became one of the most well-known and commonly deployed BattleMechs. The Archer was a contemporary of 'Mechs such as the Thunderbolt and the Banshee, and its primary job was fire support, though early in its combat career it was regarded an attack 'Mech capable of long-range brawling, and it was deployed in a variety of different duties. More than 100,000 Archers were built between its introduction and the outbreak of the First Succession War, with tens of thousands of variation types produced over the centuries. Though many were destroyed or deconstructed for spare components, they remained a common sight in the Great House troops, with six distinct manufacturers creating new Archers throughout the Succession Wars. The retrieval of the Helm Memory Core had a significant impact on the creation of the Archer. New variations were created that used rediscovered lostech, giving the venerable 'Mech a few new tricks to surprise its opponents. By the time of the Clan Invasion, most of the major manufacturers had switched over to building these new variants for the Free Worlds League, Federated Commonwealth, Free Rasalhague Republic, and Wolf's Dragoons; only Vandenberg Mechanized Industries continued to build original ARC-2R Archers for the Taurian Concordat. As technology advanced, newer, more advanced models of the Archer were produced.
BMArcher_ARCAGC.deployment: This Republic of the Sphere variant started with an ARC-4M and replaced the engine with a 280-rated light engine and replaced the arm mounted lasers with ER versions. The rear-firing lasers were removed entirely. A C3 slave unit was mounted in the head. The LRM-20s were replaced by Extended LRM-15s with three tons of ammo per launcher. This ammunition was carried in CASE II protected bays in the torso.

Two Clan-spec LRM-20s are equipped with Artemis V Fire Control Systems, and carry two tons for each launcher in a CASE II protected bay in each torso. The medium lasers were swapped for Clan spec versions and are tied into a Targeting Computer. Fourteen double heat sinks control the heat, and it's protected by thirteen tons of armor.

BMAwesome_AWS9QEarly.overview: The Awesome was designed in 2665 for the Star League by the Technicron Conglomorate and was based on the aging STR-2C Striker. While not as fast as its forefather, the Awesome is a strong assault 'Mech.
BMAwesome_AWS9QEarly.capabilities: The Awesome's abilities are nearly entirely dependent on its particle projection cannons. The AWS can withstand a severe and consistent onslaught from its weaponry thanks to several heat sinks. With one and a half tons more armor than the Striker, the AWS is more protected than even the BattleMaster. The Awesome, like any other BattleMech, has weaknesses. While it is lethal at range, it is less effective in close-quarters combat since its PPCs have a tougher time connecting with the victim. In that case, it just possesses a light weapon and a left fist to fall back on. Because of its limited mobility, it is vulnerable to flanking attacks from speedier opponents seeking to get past the PPCs. While they face arguably of the heaviest rear armor found on any BattleMech, the Awesome's lack of rear facing armaments or a weapon mount on its left arm has offered numerous MechWarriors a fighting chance. Formations of Awesomes (or even just a few) are incredibly effective and tough to stop or defeat when correctly placed by commanders who are knowledgeable of the AWS's limitations.
BMAwesome_AWS9QEarly.history: Awesomes can be found in every military House.�Because the Free Worlds League has the last remaining manufacturing factories capable of generating the Awesome, it also has the most Awesomes of any of the Great Houses. Opinions on the 'Mech vary greatly within the League. Many people laud its powers, while others believe that its limited mobility is too high a price to pay for what it can do. Similar sentiments exist in the other Houses, but none of them can debate the issue beyond academics. Few commanders would turn down the opportunity to acquire an Awesome for their army. The Awesome is typically used to assault a fixed position or to breach the enemy's line of defense. They are also in demand for defensive operations. MechWarriors piloting the Awesome can expect to be involved in heavy battle and to be charged with obtaining or guarding the most crucial objectives from the adversary.
BMAwesome_AWS9QEarly.deployment: A basic upgrade to the original 8Q introduced in 3057, the heat sinks are upgraded to double heat sinks and are reduced to nineteen. The saved weight is used to add a fourth PPC to the 'Mech as well as a Guardian ECM Suite. Although there was some criticism for using basic PPCs and not upgrading to ER models, the 9Q proved itself during Operation Guerrero to be quite devastating.
BMAwesome_AWS9QEarly.manufacturer: L�neburg Vehicle Corporation,Irian BattleMechs Unlimited,Technicron Manufacturing

BMAwesome_AWSEEPY.deployment: The Awesome has a limited, but effective, armament consisting of three Kreuss PPCs, one mounted in its right arm and two split between the left and right torsos. These give it an immense amount of damage potential at ranges exceeding five hundred meters, enough to destroy some 'Mechs with a single salvo, although at point-blank range they are less useful. For close combat the 'Mech mounts in its head a Diverse Optics Type 10 Small Laser while its left arm ends in a battlefist sufficient for hand-to-hand fighting.

BMAwesome_EEPY.overview: Introduced in 2665 by the Technicron Manufacturing Conglomerate for the Star League Defense Force, the Awesome was designed as a replacement for the aging STR-2C Striker. An eighty-ton assault 'Mech built around a punishing PPC-based armament, the Awesome earned a fearsome reputation as a durable fire-support platform capable of anchoring any battle line. Its near-total independence from ammunition resupply and notoriously difficult-to-kill design have made it one of the most valued assault 'Mechs among logistics officers and field commanders alike. Sought after by every Great House and mercenary command with the means to acquire one, the design has remained a mainstay of assault lances for over four centuries.
BMAwesome_EEPY.capabilities:
The standard AWS-8Q carries three Kreuss PPCs as its primary armament: one mounted in the right arm and two split between the left and right torsos. This battery delivers devastating firepower at ranges exceeding five hundred meters, enough to cripple most targets with a single concentrated volley. A Diverse Optics Type 10 Small Laser mounted in the head provides a token close-range weapon, while the left arm terminates in a battlefist for hand-to-hand engagements. A Pitban 240 fusion engine gives the Awesome a top speed of 64.8 kilometers per hour, adequate for an assault 'Mech but insufficient to dictate engagement range against faster opponents.

Twenty-eight standard heat sinks give the Awesome remarkable staying power in prolonged engagements, allowing MechWarriors to maintain a steady two-three-two firing cycle without significant heat buildup. The design carries fifteen tons of standard armor, exceeding even the BattleMaster in total protection, and the placement of critical systems away from explosive components makes the Awesome notoriously difficult to achieve a kill against. Rear armor coverage is among the heaviest found on any assault 'Mech, frustrating attempts by flanking units to exploit the typical weak point.

The Awesome is not without significant drawbacks, however. Its PPCs suffer from the minimum range limitations inherent to the weapon type, making close-quarters combat a serious vulnerability. With only the small laser and a single fist to fall back on at short range, the design is poorly equipped to handle opponents that close inside its effective engagement envelope. The lack of any weapon mount on the left arm further limits its options. The 'Mech's modest ground speed leaves it vulnerable to flanking maneuvers by faster opponents seeking to bypass the forward PPC arc, and commanders who fail to account for these limitations often find their Awesomes isolated and overwhelmed.
BMAwesome_EEPY.history:
Technicron Manufacturing developed the Awesome on Savannah in the Free Worlds League, delivering the first production units to the SLDF in 2665 as a direct replacement for the aging Striker. The design proved itself quickly, and by the time of the Amaris Coup the Awesome had secured a place in front-line regiments across the Star League. When the SLDF departed on the Exodus, significant numbers of Awesomes remained behind with the Successor State militaries, setting the stage for the design's long career in the wars to come.

The Succession Wars placed enormous strain on Awesome production. Technicron's factory complex on Savannah survived the early conflicts largely intact, making it the sole remaining source of new Awesomes by the end of the Second Succession War. This gave the Free Worlds League a decisive advantage in fielding the design, and League diplomats occasionally leveraged Awesome sales as a bargaining tool with other Houses. The loss of advanced manufacturing knowledge during the wars had little effect on the base AWS-8Q, whose reliance on standard components and lack of advanced technology made it one of the easier assault 'Mechs to maintain and produce. Field technicians valued the design for its repairability, and many Awesomes accumulated centuries of continuous service through careful maintenance and component swaps.

The recovery of Star League technology in the 3040s opened new possibilities for the Awesome chassis. Technicron introduced the AWS-9M in 3049, incorporating an XL engine, double heat sinks, and ER PPCs to bring the venerable design into the modern era. The Clan Invasion further accelerated development, and the following decades saw a steady stream of new variants incorporating increasingly advanced technology. Irian BattleMechs Unlimited eventually partnered with other manufacturers on several later models, broadening the Awesome's production base beyond Savannah for the first time in centuries. Through the Jihad and the Dark Age, the Awesome continued to evolve, with Technicron's Savannah facility remaining the primary production center even as new factories on other worlds began contributing their own specialized variants.
BMAwesome_EEPY.deployment:
This variant is the standard production model and the most widely encountered variant across the Inner Sphere. Originally fielded by Star League Defense Force line regiments, the base Awesome saw service in every major theater of the Reunification War's aftermath and the early Succession Wars. Its straightforward all-energy loadout made it a natural choice for garrison commands and defensive formations where ammunition resupply was uncertain. SLDF units frequently deployed Awesomes in pairs or lances to create overlapping fields of PPC fire, a tactic adopted by the Successor States and still employed by assault formations to this day.

The Free Worlds League maintained the highest concentration of AWS-8Qs due to its control of the Savannah production lines, and League commanders came to treat the design as the backbone of their heavy assault formations. Other Houses acquired their Awesomes through battlefield salvage, trade, and the occasional raid on League supply depots, ensuring that the 8Q could be found in virtually every major military force across the Inner Sphere.

BMBlackHawkKU_BHKUOBrousse.overview:
The Black Hawk-KU is an Inner Sphere OmniMech born from the Draconis Combine's crash program to reverse-engineer captured Clan technology following the Clan Invasion. After salvaging numerous examples of the Nova from the fighting on Luthien in 3052, Combine scientists discovered that, unlike most Clan designs, the Nova employed standard-grade materials for its chassis and armor rather than compact Clan-exclusive composites. Engineers at Luthien Armor Works realized they could replicate this design far more readily than other Clan OmniMechs.

The resulting Black Hawk-KU, introduced in 3055, required a ten-ton weight increase to sixty tons in order to mount heavier Inner Sphere weaponry while maintaining comparable performance to its Clan progenitor. Despite this compromise, the design proved swift, flexible, and effective. Licensing agreements spread production across multiple factories in the Federated Suns and Lyran Alliance, making the Black Hawk-KU one of the most widely produced Inner Sphere OmniMechs of its generation.
BMBlackHawkKU_BHKUOBrousse.capabilities:
Built on an Alshain 52 Endo Steel chassis and powered by a Vlar 300 XL engine, the Black Hawk-KU matches the Nova's top speed of eighty-six kilometers per hour despite its additional ten tons of mass. Five Icarus Standard jump jets provide the same one-hundred-fifty-meter jump range as its Clan cousin, ensuring the design retains the mobility that makes the Nova chassis family so tactically flexible. Twelve and a half tons of Mitchel Argon armor provide near-maximum protection for the chassis weight, and fourteen double heat sinks manage the waste heat common to its various configurations across seventeen tons of available pod space.

In its Primary configuration, the Black Hawk-KU mounts six medium lasers, four medium pulse lasers, and two small lasers, aping the Nova Prime's emphasis on close-range energy weapon saturation. While the Inner Sphere weapons lack the range and hitting power of their Clan counterparts, they generate significantly less waste heat. This means the Black Hawk-KU is particularly less prone to heat-triggered shutdowns after an alpha strike, a practical advantage that partially compensates for the reduced per-weapon lethality.

The pod technology allows pilots and commanders to tailor configurations to mission requirements and personal preferences. Some loadouts replicate Clan originals while others play to Inner Sphere weapons strengths. Independent analysis in What 'Mech? magazine comparing Luthien Armor Works and Coventry Metal Works production favors the Steiner variant, citing superior manufacturing processes and tighter quality control, though both producers deliver functionally identical designs using locally sourced equipment.
BMBlackHawkKU_BHKUOBrousse.history:
The Black Hawk-KU was initially deployed with the Legion of Vega for field tests against Clan Smoke Jaguar, where the Inner Sphere copies proved their resilience against their smaller but more advanced Clan cousins. These early engagements validated the design concept and demonstrated that Inner Sphere engineering could produce a competitive OmniMech platform. The design went on to serve with distinction during Operations Bird Dog and Bulldog, the campaign to liberate the Smoke Jaguar occupation zone, where captured Clan weapon pods were even mounted on some Black Hawk-KUs to bolster task force firepower.

The Black Hawk-KU saw further combat during the First Combine-Dominion War against the Ghost Bears and in operations against the Federated Suns during the FedCom Civil War. These conflicts also resulted in several examples falling into enemy hands. The Ghost Bears especially employ captured Black Hawk-KUs equipped with Clan weapon pods in their second-line clusters, while the handful acquired by the Armed Forces of the Federated Suns serve within the Brigade of Guards.

Prior to the FedCom Civil War, only units within the Lyran State Command received the Coventry Metal Works production models. The licensing deal, concluded at the First Whitting Conference, also brought StarCorps Industries into the program with facilities on Crofton, Loburg, Son Hoa, and St. Ives. The civil conflict hampered manufacturing, but a significant number of licensed models still entered service. The Lyran Alliance Armed Forces maintained a particularly large contingent thanks to the Coventry and Loburg production lines. In stark contrast to the limited numbers employed by the AFFS, the LAAF's access to multiple domestic producers ensured a steady supply throughout the Jihad and into the Dark Age.

The Black Hawk-KU remains in production at multiple facilities across the Inner Sphere as of the early thirty-second century, though the Luthien facility's output has been disrupted by the successive upheavals that have struck the Draconis Combine. The design's widespread adoption across multiple factions confirms the soundness of the original Luthien Armor Works engineering team's decision to base their reverse-engineering effort on the Nova's accessible standard-technology construction.
BMBlackHawkKU_BHKUOBrousse.deployment: The Primary configuration carries six medium lasers, four medium pulse lasers, and two small lasers, mirroring the Nova Prime's emphasis on close-range energy weapon saturation with Inner Sphere hardware. While individual weapons hit softer than their Clan equivalents, the Black Hawk-KU generates far less waste heat during a full alpha strike, allowing more aggressive fire patterns without risking shutdown. This configuration sees widespread use across all producing factions and remains the most commonly encountered loadout in garrison and frontline units alike.
BMBlackHawkKU_BHKUOBrousse.manufacturer: Luthien Armor Works - OmniMech Production Facility|Coventry Metal Works|Corean Enterprises|StarCorps Industries|StarCorps Industries|StarCorps Industries|StarCorps Industries|Victory Industries (formerly Norse BattleMech Works)

BMBlackHawk_Prime.overview: The Nova is a versatile medium OmniMech that is capable of acting as a workhorse design in most forces. In fact, the design is so well balanced that it inspired the creation of the Black Hawk-KU, an Inner Sphere design based on the Nova and named using the Inner Sphere code name for this 'Mech: Black Hawk.
BMBlackHawk_Prime.capabilities: Equipped with a 250 XL Model SF-2 Fusion Engine, the Nova has a ground speed of 86.4 km/h that allows it to keep up with most heavy and assault 'Mechs while being outpaced by most lighter 'Mechs. However, the Nova remains very maneuverable through the use of five jump jets, allowing the Nova to jump up to 150 meters. The fourteen double heat sinks that the Nova mounts are barely sufficient to handle its heavy heat load of its variants. The Nova is also protected by ten tons of armor, providing the 'Mech with ninety-six percent of its maximum armor protection.
BMBlackHawk_Prime.history: The Black Hawk's debut battle was the combined assault on Kindraa Smythe-Jewel on Foster in 2872 launched by the Hell's Horses, Clan Coyote and Kindraa Payne that saw the Smythe-Jewels wiped out as a faction. However, the number of Novas has been diminishing. Only spare parts have been in production since 2921. Designed as an early OmniMech by Clan Hell's Horses, the Nova was produced at the famous Tokasha Mechworks beginning in 2870. At the time of its introduction, the Nova was designed for infantry support, and was the first OmniMech designed with hardpoints allowing Elementals to easily mount and dismount the chassis. However, after Tokasha was taken by Clan Ghost Bear during the Battle of Tokasha, production of the Nova ceased. Since 2921, every Clan has acquired Nova OmniMechs as isorla. Although the last Nova factory was replaced to create Pouncers, the very few remaining in Clan Space have gotten into the hands of Aggressor Mechwarriors.
BMBlackHawk_Prime.deployment: In its primary configuration the Nova is a devastating close combat 'Mech and battle armor killer that is capable of delivering a powerful barrage with its twelve ER Medium Lasers. To help it use these as effectively as possible, the Nova has added four more double heat sinks bringing the total up to eighteen. Even with the added double heat sinks, the Nova prime is capable of producing almost twice as much heat as it can dissipate.

BMBlackKnight_BL9KNTRei.overview: The Black Knight was introduced in 2578 both as a front-line combatant and as a command BattleMech at the company and battalion level for the Star League Defense Force.
BMBlackKnight_BL9KNTRei.capabilities: Thirteen tons of armor protection on an Endo Steel frame allows the Black Knight to withstand significant punishment. The biggest disadvantage of the design is its challenging heat curve, though the fact that it mounts fifteen double heat sinks ensures that the Black Knight can still disperse a significant amount of heat produced by its weapons. However, if the 'Mech is involved in a prolonged combat situation, a less experienced MechWarrior who is not competent at heat management may be in trouble. The Black Knight's superior communications equipment allowed it to readily coordinate a whole company of 'Mechs at the same time, as well as link together the command frequencies of an entire parent regiment and connect with orbital support satellites. Improved construction materials provided the Black Knight with a light but sturdy chassis capable of carrying more weaponry and armor for less weight. With the type and amount of weapons carried by the 'Mech, the only challenge the Black Knight faces is heat management.
BMBlackKnight_BL9KNTRei.history: The Black Knight first debuted in the Reunification War when they were fielded by the 3rd Heavy Assault Regiment in combat with the Taurian Concordat; following its success it was soon adopted by I Corps and the rest of the SLDF. When the Star League collapsed, Kong Interstellar continued producing Black Knights until their Connaught orbital factory was destroyed in 2802. In 2809, Kong was able to restart their production line, but was no longer capable of producing many of the more advanced components found in the Black Knight. As a result, Kong was forced to design a downgraded version, the BL-7-KNT. Finally the Black Knight production line was permanently destroyed in a raid by the Capellan Confederation in 2820, reducing the company to producing low-grade spare parts and performing maintenance. During the Succession Wars many Black Knights fell into the hands of the Great Houses, although as the years progressed they were increasingly forced to repair them with inferior components and their numbers slowly decreased. Some were modified with a number of nonstandard components such as jump jets but few if any survived into the thirty-first century. ComStar's stockpiles of Star League 'Mechs included the Black Knight, which they found to be an excellent command unit for Level II and Level III units when the Com Guards were formed, but the very few Black Knights gifted to the Draconis Combine during Operation ROSEBUD was apparently the result of a clerical error. For their part the Clans maintained the Black Knight, although by the time of the Clan Invasion it was reserved for solahma units. The Black Knight was one of several Clanbusters put together by ComStar leading up to the Battle of Tukayyid, and in 3063 Robinson Standard BattleWorks secured a license from Kong Interstellar to build a new variant, the BL-12-KNT. Two years later KIC finished rebuilding their plant and began producing Black Knights of their own.
BMBlackKnight_BL9KNTRei.deployment: One of the so-called "Clanbuster" refits developed by ComStar in the lead-up to the Battle of Tukayyid, this variant has been equipped with an Vlar 300 XL engine, allowing it to mount an upgraded and heavier weapons load. The PPC was upgraded to a Magna Sunspot ER PPC, the medium lasers switched to Aberdovey Medium Pulse Lasers, an Aberdovey Large Pulse Laser added to the center torso, and both the small laser and Beagle Active Probe removed. To handle the heavier heat load, the heat sinks were upgraded to double strength versions. In addition, for close-in work, the Black Knight carries long-sword shaped 5-ton Hatchet in its left hand, allowing it to make devastating physical attacks.

As a result, Kong was forced to design a downgraded version, the BL-7-KNT. Finally the Black Knight production line was permanently destroyed in a raid by the Capellan Confederation in 2820, reducing the company to producing low-grade spare parts and performing maintenance. During the Succession Wars many Black Knights fell into the hands of the Great Houses, although as the years progressed they were increasingly forced to repair them with inferior components and their numbers slowly decreased. Some were modified with a number of nonstandard components such as jump jets but few if any survived into the thirty-first century. ComStar's stockpiles of Star League 'Mechs included the Black Knight, which they found to be an excellent command unit for Level II and Level III units when the Com Guards were formed, but the very few Black Knights gifted to the Draconis Combine during Operation ROSEBUD was apparently the result of a clerical error. For their part the Clans maintained the Black Knight, although by the time of the Clan Invasion it was reserved for solahma units. The Black Knight was one of several Clanbusters put together by ComStar leading up to the Battle of Tukayyid, and in 3063 Robinson Standard BattleWorks secured a license from Kong Interstellar to build a new variant, the BL-12-KNT. Two years later KIC finished rebuilding their plant and began producing Black Knights of their own.
BMBlackKnight_BLKNT4DRei.deployment: This version is equipped with an ER PPC, two ER Medium Pulse Lasers, two ER small lasers, and two small pulse lasers, all made to Clan specifications. This Black Knight also carries a plasma rifle in each arm, which use three tons of ammunition. The Medium Shield and thirteen and a half tons of armor protect the Endo-Composite structure. An Angel ECM Suite provides electronic warfare support, and eighteen Clan-spec double heat sinks keep the 'Mech running cool. Its max speed is 54 km/h.

BMBlackLanner_ERSL.overview: Newly developed by Clan Jade Falcon for the Clan Invasion of the Inner Sphere, the Black Lanner is a fast medium OmniMech that can take on a variety of roles.
BMBlackLanner_ERSL.capabilities: Intended as a front-line 'Mech, the Black Lanner makes use of all weight-saving technology available to the Clan Jade Falcon scientists who designed it, including an XL Engine, Endo Steel, and eight tons of Ferro-Fibrous armor. The Black Lanner also includes a MASC system on the base chassis that enables it to run up to 151 km/h in bursts, allowing it to outrun many 'Mechs half its size. The design sacrifices pod space for that speed however, with an engine almost half the Black Lanner's weight leaving a mere thirteen tons for offensive weaponry.
BMBlackLanner_ERSL.history: Its development triggered from the lessons learned from the Jade Falcon touman's performance during the Placement Trials for Operation REVIVAL, delays with the initial models meant the Black Lanner only arrived in the Inner Sphere with the returning Falcon Khans following the selection of ilKhan Ulric Kerensky, first seeing action during the Battle of Tukayyid as fire-support units in light reconnaissance Stars. Since its initial deployment, the Black Lanner remained primarily a Jade Falcon design, with Clan Ice Hellion and Steel Viper also using a sizable number acquired due to numerous battles and trials between rival Clans since Tukayyid. While used for independent operations, the Black Lanner is most frequently seen in conjunction with lighter designs. Mixed Stars of Black Lanners, Fire Falcons and Fire Moths are common, with the lighter 'Mechs acting as spotters and harassers for the heavier 'Mechs, flushing out the opposing units for the Black Lanner to move in for the kill. This synergistic relationship is similar to that shared between the smaller indigenous Fire Falcon and the introduced black lanners on Ironhold, both birds benefiting from the presence of the other. Originally built on the Jade Falcon homeworld of Ironhold, production would also eventually begin in the Inner Sphere to mitigate the distance to the Clan Homeworlds. By the dawn of the Wars of Reaving, production of the Black Lanner would cease in Clan Space by 3068 as tensions between the Falcons and the Homeworld Clans increased.
BMBlackLanner_ERSL.deployment: The primary configuration of the Black Lanner is effective at all ranges. At long range, it can make use of an ER Large Laser and LRM-10. When it closes in, it can attack with a pair of ER Medium Lasers and an SRM-6 that can exploit holes in its opponent's armor. An ECM Suite protects it from many forms of electronics.

BMCataphract_CTF1XRebuild.overview: The Cataphract is a heavy BattleMech and one of the first original designs since the start of the Succession Wars.
BMCataphract_CTF1XRebuild.capabilities: The Cataphract, with its armor layers, can easily absorb damage, but it can also deal it. The CeresArms Smasher PPC (taken from the Vindicator) and the SarLon MaxiCannon combine to deliver a brutal punch at greater ranges. A quartet of CeresArms medium lasers close in to give short-range firepower to both the front and rear.
BMCataphract_CTF1XRebuild.history: Desperate for a heavy 'Mech design that could be produced and maintained sustainably by their deteriorating infrastructure, the Capellan Confederation built the Cataphract in 3025 to be assembled from pieces they could still manufacture. The Cataphract, dubbed a FrankenMech for its appearance and built with parts from the Marauder, Shadow Hawk, and Phoenix Hawk, was destined to become the Confederation's new standard heavy 'Mech. It entered service just before the Fourth Succession War, when the Tikonov production line was taken over by the Federated Suns. This humiliating defeat was exacerbated when numerous Davion troops, especially the Crucis Lancers, adopted the Cataphract as their own. Tikonov's product quality, on the other hand, left the Federated Suns wanting (at least in part due to deliberate obstruction by workers still loyal to the Confederation). The Confederation finally restored the lost plant with one on Betelgeuse in 3032, building their own Cataphracts in time for the Andurien Crisis. Shortly after seizing Tikonov, the Federated Commonwealth began development of the CTF-3D, which was designed to compete with an attack 'Mech while being as adaptable as feasible. Despite the modifications, the CTF-3D was only used sparingly by the Armed Forces of the Federated Commonwealth before being consigned to the Crucis March Militia as an affront to the Capellans. When the Clan Invasion began, everything that was still in production, including the Cataphract, was rushed to the front lines. The Capellans, for their part, were able to manufacture the CTF-3D from stolen schematics and went on to create their own variant, the CTF-3L, which would go on to account for 25% of all newly acquired heavy 'Mechs during the 3050s. The design's popularity would decrease in the years that followed, while a few new varieties would continue to be created as new technologies and demands arose.
BMCataphract_CTF1XRebuild.deployment: The original Cataphract, the 1X model is armed with a Ceres Arms Smasher PPC as its primary weapon, backed up by a SarLon MaxiCannon autocannon/10 with one ton of ammo and the four Ceres Arms medium lasers. Built before the discovery of the Helm Memory Core, it lacks any advanced Star League technology and is built with a standard VOX 280 engine. The 'Mech also lacks the jump jets of the 3D model.

BMCatapult_C.overview: The Catapult, though a well-designed BattleMech, has had a tumultuous existence. Produced by Hollis Incorporated in record numbers for the SLDF, the Terran Hegemony ultimately decided to forgo contract renewal, leaving Hollis with a BattleMech line but no customer. When the facility was eventually retooled to produce the BattleMaster, they chose to completely abandon their Catapult chassis, and its numbers began to decline. The attrition of the Succession Wars left the Draconis Combine with many Catapults but no parts for them. Contracting Yori Mech Works, a production line was eventually built and new Catapults began entering the DCMS by 3033. The Capellan Confederation would follow suit and the Catapult would finally claim the popularity that it deserved.
BMCatapult_C.capabilities: Carrying two LRM launchers in its "ears" and four medium lasers in its body, the Catapult was a fire support 'Mech akin to the slightly larger Archer. The inclusion of jump jets, though, and the placement of the lasers allowed many Catapults to be their own bodyguards. Many Catapult MechWarriors choose to fire all of their LRM missiles at their chosen targets before closing in to exploit any armor breaches with their lasers. This does make the Catapult slightly more flexible than many traditional support 'Mechs, although its limited ammunition stores can hamper it in extended ranged battles.
BMCatapult_C.deployment: While visible in every military of note, the Capellan Confederation and Draconis Combine would lay claim to the lion's share of the design. When production for the Catapult began in both states, the divide only increased, with many states losing all of their examples of the design by the conclusion of the Jihad. A rarely seen modification, the CPLT-C4 and its larger LRM racks left this variant with little in terms of secondary weaponry. Mostly found in Capellan forces, the creation of the Trinity Alliance and the reappearance of advanced military hardware within the Confederation would give House Liao the luxury to sell most CPLT-C4's to their new periphery allies. Regardless, this variant would not survive the ravages of the Jihad, and it has not been seen for some time.

BMCauldronBorn_EN.overview: Introduced in 3049 to address flaws in the venerable Hellbringer, by Operation REVIVAL the Ebon Jaguar had only just started to spread through the Jaguar's touman with the Inner Sphere first encountering the design during the Battle of Luthien.
BMCauldronBorn_EN.capabilities: The primary configuration of the Ebon Jaguar was made to attack from long range. With a mammoth Gauss Rifle occupying the entire right arm, a 75mm LB 5-X AC in the left arm, and an LRM-10 jutting above the birdlike shoulder, it can act as a speedy support 'Mech with a variety of ways to deal damage. An SRM-2 and an ER Medium Laser provide backup for short range work. The thirteen integral double heat sinks are more than enough to keep the OmniMech cool, while its nine and a half tons of Ferro-Fibrous armor keep the design safe.
BMCauldronBorn_EN.history: Facing it for the first time when engaging the First Jaguar Guards Cluster in the Kado-Guchi Valley, the 'Mech's ability to take damage and remain operational led the Inner Sphere warriors who fought it to call it the Cauldron-Born after the unstoppable zombies of Irish myth. Paradoxically many of the other Clans also used the Inner Sphere Cauldron-Born reporting name, having first learned detailed information of the design from Inner Sphere sources. y Operation BULLDOG the Cauldron-Born made up a large proportion of the heavy 'Mechs of the rebuilding Smoke Jaguars, and following their Annihilation the design proliferated the remaining Clans, save the Jade Falcons, Wolves and Coyotes who continued to favor the Hellbringer. Following the Wars of Reaving the Ebon Jaguar had finally supplanted the Hellbringer in most Homeworld Clans, save the Coyotes who remained loyal to redeeming the honor of the design.

BMCicada_C.overview: Designed as a supplement or replacement for the popular Locust scout �Mech, the Cicada found limited use by the Star League after its introduction in 2740.
BMCicada_C.capabilities: The Cicada's main advantages are its speed and a lack of ammunition. A gigantic Pitban 320 engine, which accounts for more than half of the Cicada's entire mass, propels the 40-ton BattleMech to almost 130 kph. A trio of lasers, two medium and one small, provide the Cicada with ample firepower to take on its chosen targets: lighter 'Mechs. The lasers are implanted in the Cicada's armored midsection, beneath the toughest shielding the 'Mech mounts, however their shooting arcs are limited. A foe who is successful in getting behind the Cicada has little to fear. The rest of the 'Mech, however, is just minimally armored. Its limbs are vestigial�really, wings�and can only carry little armor. The Cicada's legs, while robust enough to carry the 'Mech at breakneck speeds, are also unarmored.
BMCicada_C.history: The Cicada was introduced amidst rising tensions in 2740 by HartfordCo Industries in an attempt to capitalize on the growing need for BattleMechs. At the time Bergan Industries had cornered the market in light recon 'Mechs with their Locust and HartfordCo's only prior experience was building communications and targeting systems. Nevertheless the company designed their 'Mech to compete directly with the Locust by making it just as fast but twice as large, allowing the Cicada to carry slightly better weaponry and give it an edge in physical combat, all while keeping costs down. The Star League was sufficiently impressed that they agreed to a limited contract with HartfordCo for a small number of Cicadas, using them to replace many Locusts lost in fighting along its border regions. The 'Mech's only major issue was faulty heat sinks, although these were eventually replaced in many models with modular sinks. With their base of operations and only Cicada factory located on Bryant, HartfordCo was among the many victims of the fall of the Star League and the onset of the Succession Wars. Although the factory was destroyed, there were a number of built Cicadas in storage on Bryant and its relative position compared to the five Successor States meant the planet was the target of many raids. Thus the Cicada found its way into all of the major House armies, where in service to the Successor Lords, the 'Mech earned a positive reputation. Unfortunately this also meant its expectations sometimes exceeded the design's actual capabilities, and, with no new models being produced, the Cicada's numbers began to dwindle. By the end of the Fourth Succession War the 'Mech was in danger of going extinct. The discovery of the Helm Memory Core allowed Free Worlds Defense Industries to save the Cicada by restarting production of the 'Mech from their newly-refurbished production line on Gibson. The great majority of these newly-built Cicadas went to the Free Worlds League Military, particularly federal units, which were in desperate need for recon 'Mechs after the loss of so many machines during their long battle with Andurien. The chassis was also the perfect platform to apply much of the now-recovered lostech and the improved CDA-3M was introduced shortly before the start of the Clan Invasion. New Cicadas were supplied to the Federated Commonwealth and Draconis Combine as part of an agreement to combat the Clans, and as reports filtered in from the front more variants of the 'Mech were produced. Following the ComStar Schism the Word of Blake relocated to Gibson and an increasing amount of Cicada production was redirected to the Word of Blake Militia, which quickly rivaled the FWLM as the largest Cicada user. As such the 'Mech was found on both sides of the Jihad.
BMCicada_C.deployment: Two Magna medium lasers and one Magna 200 mini laser are positioned on the Cicada's torso. These armaments allow the Cicada logistical independence in exchange for a high-heat loadout. They provide the Cicada enough firepower to take out lighter 'Mechs and are shielded by the armored torso. Unfortunately, their positioning limits the lasers' shooting arc, creating a blind patch behind the Cicada. The Cicada has four tons of armor, like the Locust, but most of it is in the torso. This leaves very little in the vestigial "wing" arms or legs; speed is the Cicada's greatest defense, which it achieves thanks to its Pitban 320 fusion engine. Original CDA-2A Cicadas made by HartfordCo employed the Hartford J15 B communications system and Hartford S1000 targeting-tracking system. The Cicada has 10 single heat sinks, however the initial HartfordCo models wore down over time. Without replacement, they'd operate at 60% capacity, causing combat overheating. Most Cicadas had their heat sinks changed with more dependable modular models, although some still employed the flawed ones in a publicized combat on Oriente between the Free Worlds League and the Capellan Confederation. The FWLM didn't order upgrades for federal and provincial Cicadas until 3029.
BMCicada_C.manufacturer: HartfordCo,Gibson Federated BattleMechs (A Division of Free Worlds Defense Industries)

BMCicada_CDA9C.deployment: Part of a limited run of experimental models in 2840, the 3C variant is an attempt to allow the 'Mech to have a much greater reach with devastating results. To do this, it uses a smaller VOX 280 engine, reducing the maximum speed to 118.8 km/h. In place of the laser weapons are two machine guns in the legs and a Donal PPC in the right torso. Despite its small numbers this variant did see combat during the Succession Wars and can be found in Capellan Confederation training academies.

BMCoyotl_Luna.overview: The Coyotl has the honor of being the first OmniMech.
BMCoyotl_Luna.capabilities: Built on an endo steel chassis and powered by a Pitban 280 XL engine, these lightweight technologies allowed the Coyotl to carry a great deal of equipment. The powerful engine allowed the 'Mech to move up to 118.8 km/h, giving it the ability to avoid enemy fire as well as dash about the battlefield
BMCoyotl_Luna.history: Developed by Clan Coyote with some help from Clan Sea Fox, the Coyotl was first fielded in 2854. Taking the modular nature of the earlier Mercury to its logical conclusion, the Coyotl was originally developed along the same lines. When the Coyote scientists saw that they could create the OmniMech, they jumped at the chance. The result was a 'Mech that could have its weapons load tailored for a particular mission, though the Coyotes did not fully capitalize on Omni technology until 2859, when the Coyotes equipped all of their Alpha Galaxy with OmniMechs and expanded rapidly within the Kerensky Cluster. Though a useful 'Mech that demonstrated how effective an OmniMech could be, the Coyotl couldn't compete with later designs from other Clans. Furthermore, the Coyotl production became ever more stifled as the fortunes of Clan Coyote waned and territory was lost. With the supply of spare parts dwindling, the Coyotes shut down their production lines in 2943. Sensing a sales opportunity, the Coyotl was one of a number of Golden Century designs Clan Sea Fox would revive to sell during the Dark Age era.
BMCoyotl_Luna.deployment: The Primary configuration of the Coyotl used an ER PPC and LRM-10 as the primary long range weapons, while a medium pulse laser and Streak SRM-4 provided short range firepower. Originally the engineers thought they would build each variant around the PPC, but this proved to be too limiting, particularly with the high heat it caused. The missile systems were added to provide firepower at a lower heat cost.

BMCrab_CRB20Minovici.overview: Designed in 2719 during the last days of the Star League, the Crab was built as a medium-weight raider and guerrilla fighter for the Star League Defense Force.
BMCrab_CRB20Minovici.capabilities: Although lacking hand actuators or jump jets, it possessed good overland speed and its all-energy weapons payload was suitable for long-range missions without the prospect for resupply. The Crab also boasted a highly advanced communications system, the Dalban Series K, and was built mostly with proven parts easy to maintain. The term "Crab walk" soon became synonymous with easy duty, since technicians spent less than half the time maintaining the Crab than with other 'Mechs of the same weight class.
BMCrab_CRB20Minovici.history: Less than a thousand Crabs had been produced when the Succession Wars began. So popular was the Crab that many believed it was likely to become the standard medium 'Mech of the SLDF. Unfortunately the Amaris Civil War and the dissolution of the Star League put an end to any future plans for the Crab. Cosara Weaponries' Crab factory on Northwind was practically destroyed in 2786, one of many strategic targets attacked in the opening salvos of the First Succession War. Although dedicated technicians were able to keep portions of the assembly line open, producing what spare parts they still could to rebuild damaged Crabs, the technology used for both the original's advanced armor plating and communications system became lost. Designated as CRB-20 models, these Crabs were only slightly inferior to the original: with the destruction of the military infrastructure necessary for the Dalban Series K's more advanced functions to work, its replacement by lesser systems ironically became less of an issue. By the end of the Fourth Succession War a hundred or so Crabs were still active in the Inner Sphere and all were CRB-20s. Unbeknownst to many, ComStar had secreted away massive stockpiles of Star League technology and 'Mechs, including the Crab. When it came time to form their own military original CRB-27 Crabs were used to outfit the Com Guards, while at the same time gifting downgraded CRB-20s to the Draconis Combine during the War of 3039 as part of Operation Rosebud. Concurrently the discovery of the Helm Memory Core allowed the refit line on Northwind to start upgrading the remaining downgraded Crabs to their original specification. Unknown to anyone in the Inner Sphere was the continued existence of the Crab in the arsenal of the Clans, although by the time of the Clan Invasion these had been relegated to second-line and garrison units. Eventually Cosara Weaponries was able to restart production of the original Crab in the 3050s and brand-new variants began turning up. Among these were new CRB-30s built by ComStar (later acquired by the Word of Blake) which made use of new C3 technology; it was also rumored ComStar sold these models to the rump Free Rasalhague Republic. During the Jihad the Word of Blake managed to capture the Northwind factory and begin producing their own radical variant known as the CRB-45.
BMCrab_CRB20Minovici.deployment: The CRB-20 is a downgrade of the CRB-27 Crab, first sighted in 2810. The only major modifications to the design was the use of standard armor which reduced the overall protection on the Crab and the replacement of its electronic systems with more reliable off-the-shelf models. When ComStar gifted their Crabs to the Draconis Combine they replaced the original components with a Garret T-11b communications system and a Garret D2j targeting and tracking system.

BMCrab_CRB27slC.overview: Designed in 2719 during the last days of the Star League, the Crab was built as a medium-weight raider and guerrilla fighter for the Star League Defense Force.

BMCrab_CRB27slC.deployment: The Crab carries two RamTech 1200 Large Lasers, each housed in the claw and forearm of either arm. While excellent weapons they are quite delicate with the focusing mirrors liable to be knocked out of alignment if used in hand-to-hand combat; fixing them requires the pilot to leave their cockpit and stick their head up into each arm's elbow to make the necessary adjustments. Backing up the large lasers is a Ceres Arms Medium Laser in the center torso, well-protected from the fusion engine and with easy access to the sixteen heat sinks, and an Exostar Small Laser in the head, a last-resort weapon more suitable for fighting infantry and rioting citizens. Nine tons of Ferro-Fibrous Armor provides good protection for its size as even the weakest locations, the left and right torso, can each survive a shot from a PPC. Originally lacking hand actuators, Cosara later provided a refit kit to install one in either or both arms.

BMCyclops_CP11C4.overview: The Cyclops began production in 2710 as an assault BattleMech and headquarters unit for Star League Defense Force field commanders.
BMCyclops_CP11C4.capabilities: A decent mix of weapons provided the Cyclops with both long- and short-range firepower and a sizable engine to keep up with mobile operations. But the centerpiece of the Cyclops is its sophisticated holographic battle computer, the Tacticon B-2000. When combined with the Omstead Olmstead 840 tight beam comm suite with SatNav module, the Cyclops can effectively command a brigade across the surface of an entire world. Unfortunately the Cyclops is poorly protected relative to other assault 'Mechs with only ten tons of armor, and while its normal load of twelve heat sinks allows it to stay cool in most cases, they can prove to be inadequate in especially heavy situations.
BMCyclops_CP11C4.history: Unfortunately the Cyclops did not fare so well with the onset of the Amaris Civil War when the Rim Worlds Republic Military destroyed its primary factory on Caph in 2774. Spare parts for the machine quickly began to dry up, until by the end of the Succession Wars less than ten percent still had a working B-2000 computer. Lacking the critical part of being a command vehicle many Cyclopses were pressed into an assault role. Acting as the bodyguard or decoy for the real commanding officer, they proved to be swift if mediocre combat machines. As most enemies know about the vulnerable head and automatically target it in combat, many Cyclops pilots also began adding false head protectors to defend against impacts. A study by the NAIS after the Fourth Succession War would eventually find that these caused more harm than good by restricting vision and increasing shrapnel, ending the practice altogether. The rediscovery of the Helm Memory Core kick-started a number of attempts to replicate the B-2000 computer, however there were no successful attempts prior to the Clan Invasion. Also, the invention of the C3 Computer with its emphasis on small-scale networking, and a switch in doctrine to using DropShips as command posts, saw the Cyclops' command role decline. However Grumium Creations was eventually able to restart production of new variants of the 'Mech, and where it has participated a Cyclops with a working B-2000 has had noticeable effects. For this reason Cyclops 'Mechs with a working battle computer were reserved for high-ranking officers.
BMCyclops_CP11C4.deployment: Based on the 11-C variant and first fielded in 3073, this Cyclops is a company command-level machine that carries a pair of C3 Master Computers. To make room for these massive computers, the Gauss Rifle, its ammunition, and some armor were replaced by a single ER PPC. This leaves the Cyclops slightly undergunned for an assault 'Mech, but it shouldn't be engaging the enemy closely anyway.
