CVBehemothHeavyTank_WYSIWYG.overview: The Behemoth was designed for increased firepower and heavy armor rather than speed or maneuverability. The design allowed the Behemoth to engage both distant and short-range targets, while allowing it to stand up to almost any type of unit possible.
CVBehemothHeavyTank_WYSIWYG.capabilities: Despite the fact that the initial design plan allowed for a whopping twenty-one tons of armor, only thirteen were used to offer greater weight for weaponry. Though not as thick as originally envisioned, the protection is adequate against the majority of threats. With a peak speed of only 32 kph, even the slowest 'Mech can readily catch up to the Behemoth if it's ready to face the firepower.

systems.label: Targeting/Tracking
systems.manufacturer: Cirxese Ballista- Check with Cirxese RockeCheck

CVBehemothHeavyTank_WYSIWYG.history: House Davion's Federated Suns troops are noted for their quick deployments and quick combat strategies. AFFS opponents soon learn to expect their adversaries to arrive anywhere, at any moment. The idea of that army commissioning something as ponderous and unmaneuverable as the Behemoth Heavy Tank is unthinkable to most Davion military planners, but Aldis Industries debuted the vehicle to the AFFS in 2952. At the time, the conventional vehicle's purpose on the 'Mech-equipped battlefield was debatable. A vehicle must either be fast enough to dodge the BattleMech's weaponry or carry guns capable of threatening the armor of a 'Mech. The Aldis team's approach was to equip the Behemoth with cannons powerful enough to overpower a 'Mech while also carrying enough backup weapons to keep it safe from secondary threats. Although the AFFS provided the initial commission, Aldis has always been happy to sell to any interested customer. To date, the Behemoth has infiltrated practically every Successor State military, with the AFFS and the Draconis Combine Mustered Soldiery having the highest concentrations.
CVBehemothHeavyTank_WYSIWYG.deployment: The two turret-mounted SarLon MaxiCannon AC-10s enable the Behemoth to deal out a great deal of damage at a long distance. The four front-mounted LongFire Light Missile Racks increase this damage potential, particularly in the forward arc of fire. For closer targets, two Harvester 20K SRM-6 packs are mounted on the turret and the Behemoth boasts two ball turrets, each mounting one Harvester 2K SRM-2 and two MainFire MiniGuns. The final two Harvester 2K SRM-2 packs are mounted in a half-turret on the front of tank
CVBehemothHeavyTank_WYSIWYG.manufacturer: Aldis Industries,New Samarkand Metals,General Motors,Aldis Industries

CVDevastatorHeavyTank_Gauss.overview: Tall, squat, and using four treads, the Demolisher has a well-earned, though sometimes exaggerated, reputation as one of the most deadly vehicles on the battlefield. Designed to counteract the increasing scarcity of 'Mechs during the Succession Wars, the Demolisher became popular, especially on worlds where BattleMechs were not available.
CVDevastatorHeavyTank_Gauss.capabilities: The Demolisher is an intimidating machine with two turret-mounted heavy guns. The titanic weapons employ a well-known propellant system that combines two chemicals in suspension to propel a stream of massive shells at the unlucky target. This generated a tremendous amount of heat in older models. As a result, crew members were required to wear special coolant suits because the inside of the tank can become unbearably hot during a sustained firefight. The current model addressed the issue by directing the majority of the ejection gases through the barrels. The Demolisher is a defensive unit that works best in urban environments. The two massive autocannons can easily take down an assault 'Mech when they emerge from hiding. The vehicle's lack of secondary weapons, however, forces it to rely on friendly infantry to screen against enemy light armour and infantry.
CVDevastatorHeavyTank_Gauss.history: Aldis Industries was nearly destroyed during Aleksandr Kerensky's liberation of Terra in 2767 after being seized by Rim Worlds Republic troops. Aldis was rebuilt under the auspices of Jerome Blake's Terran reconstruction programme and has operated under the neutrality ComStar crafted for Terra.
CVDevastatorHeavyTank_Gauss.deployment: The third generation of the Demolisher, the DVE-5B Mark III, was found mainly among the defense forces of the richer planets in the Inner Sphere. It is powered by a Type 240 fusion engine and mounts some additional weapons: A Zippo Flamer in the turret, one Intek Small Laser in the front and on each side, and a front-mounted Holly SRM-6.

CVEridya_ERYOC.notes: Oft considered to multiply the issues the Eridya VTOL already displayed with its first three configs, the C configuration is even more expensive than the others by making extensive use of clan-spec equipment. The ERY-OC follows the lead of the prime by mounting a single energy weapon, in this case a clan-spec extended range large laser with an integrated targeting computer. A nose-mounted active probe allows it to cut through hostile ECM, while its own ECM suite attempts to keep it safe from enemy sensors.

CVManticoreHeavyTank_AC5.overview: The Manticore is one of the best-designed and most powerful tanks ever created, built to combat BattleMechs in support of infantry formations.
CVManticoreHeavyTank_AC5.capabilities: Although the Manticore mounts a variety of weapons to handle a variety of combat roles and ranges, and is heavily armored for a vehicle of its weight, it is not equipped to deal with the raw firepower of super-heavy vehicles such as the Demolisher or the Behemoth. Despite this, the popularity of the Manticore has remained high, even during the Succession Wars era with its use of an expensive and rare fusion engine.
CVManticoreHeavyTank_AC5.history: It was created by TechniCorp for the Star League member nations and quickly gained a reputation as a potent infantry-support weapon during the Reunification War. Following the destruction of TechniCorp's headquarters on Terra during the Amaris Coup, other weapon manufacturers absorbed TechniCorp's manufacturing plants around the Inner Sphere. The Manticore is now manufactured by six different companies, a testament to TechniCorp's robust design nearly five centuries after its creation. The Manticore's prominent, high profile, chosen to provide it superior angles of fire against towering BattleMechs, makes it clearly visible on the battlefield, for better or worse. Despite being more expensive than other frontline tanks such as the Bulldog, Manticores are frequently used to form entire companies and battalions. Many affluent lords who cannot afford a BattleMech formation will frequently pay for Manticores rather than any other conventional ground vehicle. The licensing of the vehicle to other corporations has carried the Manticore's notoriety to the most remote reaches of the Inner Sphere and the Periphery. Many have questioned Defiance Industries' choice to assist the Commonwealth's long-time foe, the Draconis Combine, in launching Manticore production on Proserpina. However, Defiance Industries has defended themselves by demonstrating that Bulldog Enterprises, a Combine weapons company, was already working to clone the vehicle for House Kurita, therefore it was better for the Commonwealth to wring every euro out of the design and keep the proceeds local.
CVManticoreHeavyTank_AC5.deployment: The tank's main weapon is the Parti-Kill Heavy Cannon PPC, backed up by a SureShot Mk VI SRM-6 rack. The SRM is mounted on top of the main turret, just above and behind the particle cannon. It is mounted on vertical and horizontal swivel mounts, giving the pack a full 120° arc of fire independent of the turret. Both PPC and SRM are supported in their respective range brackets by a Far Fire Medium Missile Rack and OMI High Burn medium laser.
CVManticoreHeavyTank_AC5.manufacturer: Quikscell Company,Quikscell Company,Majesty Metals and Manufacturing,Defiance Industries,New Earth Trading Company,Jalastar Aerospace,Bulldog Enterprises,TechniCorp,Defiance Industries

CVSRMCarrier_Armor.overview: Ever since the development of the first rocket centuries ago, military units have used mobile missile systems to provide cheap and effective fire support. Along with its LRM counterpart, the SRM Carrier serves in every military action across the Inner Sphere in a variety of shapes and forms.
CVSRMCarrier_Armor.capabilities: The chassis for missile carriers, but they are all essentially military transport vehicles, tracked being the most common, adapted to carry the huge missile launchers and loading equipment. Those adaptations generally consist of reinforcing the suspension systems and armoring the crew and critical components areas enough to survive the back blast from so many missiles. With an average top speed of 54 km/h and armor unable to survive counterbattery fire, if forced to engage in direct combat a carrier's life expectancy is measured in seconds.
CVSRMCarrier_Armor.history: Manufactured by almost every major military supplier, the modern SRM Carrier is derived from those developed in the Age of War by Quik Products Inc, the future Quikscell Company. In many cases derived a little too closely; during the Succession Wars era many manufacturers were producing little more than shoddy direct copies that fell below even Quikscell's low quality standards. After the end of the Fourth Succession War Quikscell lodged lawsuits against those manufacturing clones of Quik based carriers, resulting the closure of many smaller manufacturers and larger manufacturers ending production. While those who produced their own designs were not affected, Quikscell's elimination of much of its competitors and its offer to exchange any missile carrier not produced by them for legitimate Quikscell versions and parts made it the number one player of the missile carrier market.
CVSRMCarrier_Armor.deployment: While its chassis is identical to its LRM counterpart, the SRM Carrier mounts ten forward-firing SRM-6 launchers. Quikscell utilizes Holly brand launchers, but carriers produced by other manufacturers frequently use the launchers common to their House of origin, such as HoverTec SRMs in the Federated Suns or NCK in the Draconis Combine. While the SRM carrier must physically move to change firing angles a barrage fired from a missile carrier is a fearsome sight, and four tons of ammunition allow a carrier to keep up that rate of fire up for over a minute, but forces carriers to be tied to a functioning supply system to keep them in action for any extended period.
CVSRMCarrier_Armor.manufacturer: Joint Equipment Systems,Quikscell Company,Quikscell Company,Caliphate Airfield Alpha,Quikscell Company,Quikscell Company,Maxwell Planetary Defense Consortium,Quikscell Company,Quikscell Company,Hadrian Mechanized Industries,Quikscell Company,Aldis Industries,Amaris Armored Vehicles

WSNagaDestroyer_SLDFCV.overview: The Naga-class light destroyer once served as the backbone of the Terran Hegemony's fleet, replacing the older Essex-class in 2645. With its combination of heavy armor and firepower, the Naga was designed to be a cost-effective destroyer capable of delivering an efficient mix of capital weaponry. However, advancements in technology eventually rendered the Naga obsolete, and its demise came when the upgraded Essex-class was commissioned. Today, only three Nagas remain, but their age and structural flaws make restoration efforts impractical.
WSNagaDestroyer_SLDFCV.capabilities: Designed for close-quarters combat against other large ships, the Naga-class light destroyer boasted a heavy concentration of light naval autocannons and energy weapons. Its armament was simplified to streamline logistical needs, requiring only three types of ammunition. The ship featured a spacious cargo bay that could be utilized for storing additional ammunition or supplies for extended missions, enhancing its operational range and self-sustainability. To defend against enemy fighters, the Naga carried up to two squadrons of aerospace craft and a pair of heavy combat shuttles. However, the Naga had several critical flaws, including a lack of rearward firepower, slow acceleration, and limited light anti-fighter defenses.
WSNagaDestroyer_SLDFCV.history: The Naga-class light destroyer entered service in 2645, becoming the primary destroyer of the Terran Hegemony's navy. Its introduction marked the replacement of the older Essex-class, offering improved armor and firepower. The Naga's cost-effective design allowed it to deliver a potent mix of capital weaponry, making it a formidable force on the battlefield. For fifty years, the Naga reigned as the premier destroyer of the Hegemony fleet. However, technological advancements eventually led to the development of faster and more powerful ships. The upgraded Essex-class, commissioned in 2707 and launched in 2711, sounded the death knell for the Naga-class. The newer ships surpassed the capabilities of the Naga, rendering it obsolete in the eyes of the Hegemony navy. Today, only three Nagas are known to exist, having been mothballed since the fall of the Star League. These destroyers have remained in orbit around Neptune, largely overlooked during the conflicts for Terra. However, recent intelligence reports indicate that fanatics have gained control of these vessels and are attempting to reactivate them. Unfortunately, the Nagas suffer from extensive structural fatigue and engine flaws that have worsened over time due to their age. Restoration efforts are considered impractical and wasteful, given the high costs involved.
